content.content:
{"body":"Hi GO,
I was wondering, if the GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity should be kept or deleted because it is a very specific term.
Here is my reflection:
ICAM3 is a ligand of ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGDL:ITGB2 and CD209 receptors, so I was wondering
-if it would better to delete it, because it is too much specific, and use the parent term  "GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity" for these receptors (ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGDL:ITGB2 and CD209)  and add  in P2GO an annotation extension for ICAM3  to specify the receptor in "has input".
-or if it kept, create new go terms for the member of ICAM family, ICAM1, ICAM2, ICAM4 and ICAM5.
Of my point of view, as we have all tools to limit creation of new GO terms, with annotation extension in P2GO and GO-CAM, it would be simpler to delete it and use annotation extension to specify in "has input " of the ligand its specific receptor as we can do in P2GO and in GO-CAM than create five other new GO term.
What do you think?

content.content:
---
name: Obsoletion notice
about: This template is to notify ontology users of a term obsoletion.
title: 'Obsoletion notice: [GO ID: term label]'
labels: obsoletion
assignees: ''

---

Dear all, 

The proposal has been made to obsolete [GO ID: term label]. 
(keep one, remove others): 

* The reason for obsoletion is that the term is not clearly defined and usage has been inconsistent
*  The reason for obsoletion is that the meaning of the term is ambiguous.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a GO-CAM model.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term represent an assay and not a real GO process. 
* The reason for obsoletion is that this represents a phenotype.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that the data from the paper for which the term was requested can be accurately described using [appropriate GO term] 
* The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a gene product.
* The reason for obsoletion is that this term refers to a class of proteins.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was added in error.
* The reason for obsoletion is that more specific terms were created.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that this term was an unnecessary grouping term.
* The reason for obsoletion is that is not known to be catalyzed by any gene product.
*  The reason for obsoletion is that it more specific than the specificity of any known gene product.
* The reason for obsoletion is that there is no evidence that this function/process/component exists.
* (or add other reason)

Annotations:
There are X experimental annotations to this term by (list of impacted groups and their respective numbers of EXP and ISS annotations). 
or: There are no experimental annotations to this term.

There are X InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to this term. 
or: There are no mappings to this term.

This term is included in the XXX subset(s).
or: This term is not present in any subset.

Annotations can be viewed here: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-annotation tracker].

If obsolete term has a 'replaced by' term: (optional)
This term is being replaced by [GO term] 

If obsolete term has a 'consider' term:  (optional)
Annotations can be transferred to [suggested GO term(s)] 

Any comments can be  added to the issue: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-ontology tracker]. 
Unless objections are received by [DATE], we will assume that you agree to this change.

Thanks, 

The GO-Ontology Editors

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:0030369
name: ICAM-3 receptor activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "Combining with ICAM-3, intercellular adhesion molecule 3, and transmitting the signal from one side of the membrane to the other to initiate a change in cell activity. ICAM-3, or CD50, are constitutively expressed on monocytes, granulocytes and lymphocytes; on physiological stimulation, they become transiently phosphorylated on serine residues." [GOC:ai, GOC:signaling, ISBN:0198506732, PMID:7515813]
comment: Note that this term represents an activity and not a gene product. Consider also annotating to the molecular function terms 'cell adhesion molecule binding ; GO:0050839' and 'receptor binding ; GO:0005102' and the biological process term 'cell adhesion ; GO:0007155'.
is_a: GO:0004888 ! transmembrane signaling receptor activity
intersection_of: GO:0038023 ! signaling receptor activity
intersection_of: has_primary_input PR:000001468 ! intercellular adhesion molecule 3

content.text: Now I have what I need. Let me draft the obsoletion announcement and post it to the issue.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/announcement.md << 'EOF'
Here is the draft announcement based on the [obsoletion-notice template](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/blob/master/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/obsoletion-notice.md), with unnecessary text and the sentence about objections removed.

**Title:** Obsoletion notice: GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity

---

Dear all,

The proposal has been made to obsolete GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity.

The reason for obsoletion is that this term is more specific than the specificity of any known gene product. ICAM-3 is a ligand of the ITGAL:ITGB2, ITGAD:ITGB2, and CD209 receptors; the receptor activity of these gene products can be more accurately captured by annotating to the parent term GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity together with a `has_input` annotation extension specifying ICAM-3 (PR:000001468).

Annotations:
There are X experimental annotations to this term by (list of impacted groups and their respective numbers of EXP and ISS annotations).

There are X InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to this term.

This term is included in the XXX subset(s).

Annotations can be viewed here: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-annotation tracker].

Annotations can be transferred to GO:0004888 transmembrane signaling receptor activity, with a `has_input` annotation extension to PR:000001468 (ICAM-3).

Any comments can be added to the issue: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560.

Thanks,

The GO-Ontology Editors

---

Notes for you to fill in before sending:
- Annotation counts (experimental + ISS, by group)
- Number of InterPro2GO / UniRule / UniProtKW mappings
- Subset membership (if any)
- Link to the go-annotation tracker issue once it is filed

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/26769668791)
EOF
gh issue comment 30560 --repo geneontology/go-ontology --body-file /tmp/announcement.md
input.description: Post the draft announcement to the issue

content.text:
Drafted the obsoletion announcement for GO:0030369 ICAM-3 receptor activity using the [obsoletion-notice template](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/blob/master/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/obsoletion-notice.md) and posted it as a comment on issue #30560.

Changes made versus the template:
- Kept a single, tailored reason ("more specific than the specificity of any known gene product") tied to the ICAM-3 rationale from the thread; removed the other reason options.
- Removed the "Unless objections are received by [DATE]..." sentence as requested.
- Replaced "replaced by" wording with a "consider" suggestion pointing annotations to GO:0004888 + `has_input` PR:000001468, matching the proposal in the thread.
- Flagged the fields that still need real numbers (annotation counts, mapping counts, subsets, go-annotation tracker link) so they aren't sent with placeholders.

Comment: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30560#issuecomment-4594787881
